grammar.sentence: There ________ a book on the table.

grammar.question: Which sentence uses the plural correctly?

grammar.sentence: She ________ TV when I called her.
We ________ already eaten when they arrived.

grammar.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: He didn't went to the party.
He didn't go to the party.
He doesn't went to the party.
He not go to the party.
grammar.answer: He didn't go to the party.

grammar.sentence: This is ________ interesting book I've ever read.

grammar.question: Which sentence uses 'some' correctly?
options: Can I have some a information?
Can I have some an information?

grammar.sentence: If I ________ more time, I would travel the world.
The report ________ by the manager before the meeting.

grammar.question: Choose the sentence with the correct use of 'used to'.
options: I use to play football as a child.
I used to playing football as a child.
I used to play football as a child.
I was used to play football as a child.
grammar.answer: I used to play football as a child.

grammar.sentence: She suggested ________ to the cinema that evening.
By the time we arrived, the concert ________ already started.

grammar.question: Which sentence uses a relative clause correctly?
options: The man which called you is outside.
The man who called you is outside.
The man what called you is outside.
The man that called you are outside.
grammar.answer: The man who called you is outside.

grammar.sentence: If she ________ harder, she might have passed the exam.
He asked me where I ________ the previous night.

grammar.question: Choose the most natural sentence using inversion.
options: Never I have seen such a beautiful painting.
Never have I seen such a beautiful painting.
Never I saw such a beautiful painting.
Never seen have I such a beautiful painting.
grammar.answer: Never have I seen such a beautiful painting.

grammar.sentence: The new policy will affect ________ works in this department.

grammar.question: Which sentence correctly uses a participle clause?
options: Having finished the report, the meeting was attended by her.
Having finished the report, she attended the meeting.
She finished the report, having attended the meeting.
The meeting was attended, having finished the report.
grammar.answer: Having finished the report, she attended the meeting.

grammar.sentence: It is essential that every employee ________ the training module.

grammar.question: Choose the sentence that correctly uses a cleft structure for emphasis.
options: It was John who broke the vase.
It was John that has broken the vase.
It is John who has break the vase.
It was John which broke the vase.
grammar.answer: It was John who broke the vase.

grammar.sentence: ________ the project succeed, the company will expand its operations significantly.

grammar.question: Which sentence uses ellipsis correctly?
options: She can play guitar and he can play too.
She can play guitar and he can too.
She can play guitar and he also can.
She can play guitar and so does he.
grammar.answer: She can play guitar and he can too.

grammar.sentence: The results were ________ ambiguous as to render any conclusion premature.

grammar.question: Which is the most appropriate use of 'were' in a formal register?
options: If she was here, she would help us.
If she were here, she would help us.
If she is here, she would help us.
If she has been here, she would help us.
grammar.answer: If she were here, she would help us.

grammar.sentence: No sooner ________ she sat down than the phone rang.

listening.audioTranscript: Hi, my name is Tom. I am from London. I am 22 years old.
listening.question: Where is Tom from?

listening.audioTranscript: In today's meeting, the team agreed to push the product launch back by two weeks. The main reason was that the QA team needed more time to fix critical bugs before release.
listening.question: Why was the product launch delayed?
options: QA team needed more time to fix bugs
listening.answer: QA team needed more time to fix bugs

listening.audioTranscript: While the new tax reform is intended to reduce income inequality, critics argue that without addressing wealth concentration at the top, the reform will have a limited impact on lower-income households in the long run.
listening.question: What is the main criticism of the tax reform?
options: It is too complicated to implement
It will increase taxes for lower-income households
It doesn't address wealth concentration at the top
listening.answer: It doesn't address wealth concentration at the top

listening.audioTranscript: Although remote work has increased productivity for many employees, it has also blurred the boundaries between professional and personal life. As a result, companies are now exploring hybrid models that aim to balance flexibility with the need for in-person collaboration.
listening.question: What are companies exploring as a solution?
options: Hybrid models balancing flexibility and collaboration
listening.answer: Hybrid models balancing flexibility and collaboration

listening.audioTranscript: In light of the accelerating pace of technological innovation, it is imperative that policymakers adopt a proactive rather than reactive approach. Failure to anticipate the societal implications of emerging technologies could exacerbate existing inequalities and create challenges that are far more difficult to address retrospectively.
listening.question: What does the speaker suggest about policymakers' approach to technology?
options: They should wait to see the effects before acting
They should ban new technologies until proven safe
They should adopt a proactive rather than reactive approach
They should focus only on economic benefits
listening.answer: They should adopt a proactive rather than reactive approach

reading.passage: My name is Sara. I am 25 years old. I live in Madrid with my cat, Luna. I work in a small bookshop near my house. I love reading and cooking.
reading.question: Where does Sara work?

reading.passage: Today is Monday. The weather is sunny and warm. Tom wants to go to the park. He puts on his shoes and takes his dog for a walk.
reading.question: What does Tom do?
options: He takes his dog for a walk
reading.answer: He takes his dog for a walk

reading.passage: Anna is planning a birthday party for her son. She has invited 15 children. She ordered a chocolate cake and prepared sandwiches and juice. The party starts at 3 PM at their house.
reading.question: What did Anna order for the party?

reading.passage: Dear guests, the hotel swimming pool is open from 8 AM to 10 PM. Towels are available at the front desk. Children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult. Food and drinks are not allowed in the pool area.
reading.question: What rule applies to children under 12?
options: They must bring their own towels
They must be accompanied by an adult
They can only swim in the morning
reading.answer: They must be accompanied by an adult

reading.passage: Remote work has become increasingly common since the pandemic. While many employees appreciate the flexibility it offers, some companies are concerned about productivity and team cohesion. A recent survey found that 67% of workers prefer a hybrid model — a mix of home and office work — rather than working fully remotely or fully in-office.
reading.question: What does the survey suggest most workers prefer?
options: A hybrid model combining home and office
reading.answer: A hybrid model combining home and office

reading.passage: Street food markets have grown in popularity across European cities. They offer a diverse range of cuisines and provide an affordable dining experience. However, local restaurant owners have raised concerns, arguing that the markets create unfair competition since street vendors often pay lower taxes and have fewer overhead costs.
reading.question: Why do local restaurant owners object to street food markets?
options: Vendors have lower costs and taxes
reading.answer: Vendors have lower costs and taxes

reading.passage: Despite widespread adoption of digital health records, many healthcare systems still struggle with data interoperability — the ability of different systems to exchange and use information. This fragmentation means that a patient treated in one hospital may have their history unavailable to a specialist in another, potentially compromising care quality and leading to redundant testing.
reading.question: What is the main consequence of poor data interoperability in healthcare?
options: Hospitals spend more money on IT systems
Patients must carry physical health records
Patient care can be compromised due to inaccessible history
Doctors prefer paper records over digital ones
reading.answer: Patient care can be compromised due to inaccessible history

reading.passage: The term 'greenwashing' refers to the practice of companies making misleading environmental claims to appear more eco-friendly than they are. While consumer demand for sustainable products has grown, regulation of environmental marketing remains inconsistent across countries, making it difficult for buyers to distinguish genuine commitment from superficial branding.
reading.question: What does the passage imply is the main challenge consumers face with greenwashing?
options: There are too many eco-friendly companies
Inconsistent regulation makes it hard to identify genuine sustainability
Consumers are not interested in environmental issues
reading.answer: Inconsistent regulation makes it hard to identify genuine sustainability

reading.passage: Hannah Arendt's concept of the 'banality of evil,' introduced through her coverage of the Eichmann trial, challenged the assumption that acts of great evil require monstrous perpetrators. Arendt argued that Eichmann's crimes stemmed not from malice but from a profound absence of reflective thinking — what she termed 'thoughtlessness.' This thesis proved deeply controversial, as critics felt it risked diminishing individual moral responsibility.
reading.question: What was Arendt's central claim about Eichmann?
options: He was motivated by deep ideological hatred
He was morally insane and unable to make decisions
His crimes resulted from thoughtlessness rather than malice
He was a victim of circumstance with no real agency
reading.answer: His crimes resulted from thoughtlessness rather than malice

reading.passage: The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis posits that the language a person speaks influences — or, in its stronger form, determines — their perception of reality. While the strong version of this hypothesis has largely been rejected by linguists, its weaker formulation continues to attract empirical support, with studies showing that speakers of languages with distinct colour terms perceive colour boundaries differently than those whose languages lack such distinctions.
reading.question: What does the passage say about the strong version of the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis?
options: It is widely accepted by modern linguists
It has been largely rejected by linguists
It is supported by studies on colour perception
It only applies to languages with few colour terms
reading.answer: It has been largely rejected by linguists

pictureDescription.question: Which statement best describes the image?
options: A worker is assembling machinery on a factory floor.
A technician is inspecting equipment under poor lighting conditions.
A man is operating industrial machinery while wearing protective gear.
An engineer is conducting a safety check on a construction site.
pictureDescription.answer: A man is operating industrial machinery while wearing protective gear.

pictureDescription.question: Which statement best describes the image?
options: Two colleagues are reviewing a printed report at a desk.
A woman is presenting data to a group of seated colleagues.
A man is explaining a chart displayed on a screen to a colleague.
A team is participating in a video conference call in a boardroom.
pictureDescription.answer: A woman is presenting data to a group of seated colleagues.

pictureDescription.question: Which statement best describes the image?
options: A scientist is carefully measuring a liquid substance in a laboratory.
A researcher is recording observations while handling lab equipment.
A technician is disposing of chemical waste following safety protocols.
A student is conducting an experiment under supervision in a lab.
pictureDescription.answer: A researcher is recording observations while handling lab equipment.

pictureDescription.question: Which statement best describes the image?
options: A group of people are waiting in line at a busy transportation hub.
Commuters are boarding a train on a crowded urban platform.
Passengers are disembarking from a long-distance train at a terminal.
Travellers are queuing at an airport check-in counter.
pictureDescription.answer: Commuters are boarding a train on a crowded urban platform.

pictureDescription.question: Which statement best describes the image?
options: A chef is plating a dish with precision in a professional kitchen.
Kitchen staff are collaborating on a complex dish during a busy service.
A cook is hastily preparing multiple orders in an understaffed kitchen.
A trainee chef is receiving instructions from a head chef during preparation.
pictureDescription.answer: Kitchen staff are collaborating on a complex dish during a busy service.

speaking.prompt: Say your name, your age, and where you are from.
speaking.exampleResponse: My name is Alice. I am 24 years old. I am from Tunisia.

speaking.prompt: Describe your favourite colour and why you like it.
speaking.exampleResponse: My favourite colour is blue. I like it because it reminds me of the sea.

speaking.prompt: Describe what you usually do in the morning before going to work or school.
speaking.exampleResponse: I usually wake up at 7. I have breakfast and then I take a shower. After that I go to school.

speaking.prompt: Talk about a place you visited recently. What did you do there?
speaking.exampleResponse: Last month I visited the capital city. I went to a museum and ate local food. It was very interesting.

speaking.prompt: Do you think social media has more positive or negative effects on young people? Give two reasons.
speaking.exampleResponse: I think social media has both positive and negative effects. On the positive side, it helps people stay connected. However, it can also cause anxiety and reduce focus.

speaking.prompt: Describe a skill you have learned in the last two years and explain how it has helped you.
speaking.exampleResponse: I have learned to code in the last two years. It has helped me get a better job and solve problems more efficiently.

speaking.prompt: Some people argue that university education is no longer necessary. What is your view on this?
speaking.exampleResponse: While I acknowledge that vocational paths are increasingly viable, I believe university still offers critical thinking skills and networking opportunities that are difficult to replicate elsewhere.

speaking.prompt: Describe a situation where you had to make a difficult decision. What did you consider and what did you decide?
speaking.exampleResponse: I once had to decide whether to move abroad for a job opportunity. I considered the financial benefits and career growth, but weighed them against leaving my family. Ultimately, I decided to go.

speaking.prompt: To what extent do you think governments should regulate the use of artificial intelligence?
speaking.exampleResponse: Regulation is necessary to prevent abuse, particularly in areas like surveillance and automated decision-making, yet overly prescriptive frameworks risk stifling innovation. A principles-based approach that balances accountability with flexibility seems most prudent.

speaking.prompt: Discuss the relationship between economic growth and environmental sustainability. Can they coexist?
speaking.exampleResponse: The conventional view positions growth and sustainability as incompatible, yet proponents of green economics argue that decoupling GDP from resource consumption is achievable through circular economy models and clean technology investment.
